news.title: The S&P 500 Just Had Its Best Month Since 2020. Don’t Let ‘Sell in May’ Spook You.
news.summary: There was a lot that could have made the stock market fall.  Investors shrugged off questions about when—or even if—the Federal Reserve would cut interest rates again this year after the central bank held rates steady, and it ignored the fact that Jerome Powell plans to stay on the Fed’s Board of Governors even as Kevin Warsh prepares to become chair. Inflation came in hot as oil prices continued to rise, and earnings from  Microsoft  and  Meta Platforms  revealed sizable capital spending increases—and resulted in falling stocks.  Eli Lilly  earnings offered evidence that weight-loss drugs were only getting bigger;  Coca-Cola’s  beat showed that not all soft-drink makers are suffering from an Ozempic overhang;  Alphabet’s  capital spending paid off with big profits; and  Caterpillar’s  numbers demonstrated that there’s more to its business than mining and machinery.

news.title: Bam Azizi, Mesh CEO: A Fortt Knox Conversation
news.summary: Mesh CEO Bam Azizi told Jon Fortt his thesis: crypto is "built by crypto bros for crypto bros" and doesn't pass the grandma test. Mesh — fresh off a $75M Series C at $1B led by Dragonfly — abstracts wallet/chain fragmentation so users tap-and-pay. Born in Iran during the Iran-Iraq war, PhD from TU Munich, sold prior startup NoPassword to LogMeIn (2019). Mesh pivoted from B2C to B2B in 2022, post-FTX. Big bet: agentic commerce + microtransactions.

news.title: S&P 500 and Nasdaq Start May at Fresh Record Highs
news.summary: Stocks started off May with some modest gains after the S&P 500 and Nasdaq wrapped their best month since 2020. The Dow rose 332 points, or 0.7%. The S&P 500 was up 0.8%. The Nasdaq was up 1%. The S&P 500 and Nasdaq each set fresh intraday records shortly after the open.

news.title: S&P Is Thinking About Changing Its Rules for the S&P 500. What It Means for SpaceX, OpenAI.
news.summary: SpaceX, Anthropic and OpenAI could all be fast-tracked into the S&P 500 after their potential IPOs under new rules proposed late Thursday.  S&P Dow Jones Indices unveiled a number of potential changes to its eligibility criteria and rules for so-called Megacap companies, in a document published late Thursday.  Megacap companies would also be exempt from financial viability criteria requiring them to be profitable before being included in S&P indexes.
